channel.title: /biz/ Active (filtered, with replies)
channel.description: Filtered out: general, gme, bbbyq. OP + first 3 replies + last 30 replies.

item.title: If you’ve held Chainlink for years, ask yourself what you’ve been waiting for. — 109 replies — 436.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
If you’ve held Chainlink for years, ask yourself what you’ve been waiting for.

Open thread • Replies: 109 • Updated: 2026-06-22 14:57 UTC

OP

If you’ve held Chainlink for years, ask yourself what you’ve been waiting for.

LINK is a $5.7B token on $57.5M of annual fees. That’s ~99x revenue, and it has been flat for years, straight through every “banks are coming” cycle. The promise was always that adoption converts into value. It hasn’t. It’s been “soon” since 2020.

So here’s the question no diehard wants to answer:

What fee number, by what date, would make you sell? If LINK still earns ~$57M in 2028, is the thesis wrong, or is it still “soon”? If you can’t name the number and the date, you’re not investing in Chainlink. You’re believing in it. And belief has no exit.

Early replies (first 3)

62382825
never. Its not about the link token, its just about the hope for the future it gives. That is what keeps me going, even if its a delusion. Thus I will never sell, because as long as I hold the token that fantastical delusion can still be real in my mind

62382842
chatgpt post

62382990
>>62382842
OP is a mentally ill faggot

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62395706
>>62382825
It is literally an entrenched jewish rent seeking project minted on a completely arbitrary blockchain which INCREASES frictions intentionally for no reason to collect a fee, and it is totally unnecessary and 100x slower and more expensive than its competitors to use. The entire bet is that it will be used for some reason and that institutional consortium trust will be continually important a decade after it became technically irrelevant and automate-able. Link is down 90% from ATH, has performed worst than LUNC since the Trump pump and is already RIDICULOUSLY overpriced, not being able to support 30% of the network's operation from revenues and has zero road or vector to accrue actual value or revenue to token holders in any meaningful way, meanwhile Sirgay literally pays just sells directly to them directly from the operator reward pool that was supposed to be ringfenced routinely whenever they make an "announcement" that they will be used for price feeds which mean NOTHING to token holders. It is so cynical and low IQ and transparently extractive and fraudulent it is literally unbelievable.

62395713
>>62382990
>>62382842
>>62383251
>>62383267
see
>>62395706

62395728
>>62395706
>more expensive than its competitors to use
I wouldn't exactly say that lmao

Keep trying though, verbose cripplet.

62395748
>>62383932
>>62383267
>>62388670
DTCC literally said it will be more
than 15 fucking years to rollout the partnered product at Smartcon, when Sirgay tried to prompt excitement and the DTCC rep tamped down the fake hype he was trying to manufacture to sell on the clip of like he always does. SWIFT were SO negligent and behind the curve on DLT they fired their Chief Innovation Officer who probably squandered the biggest moat in industrial history or at least since Blockbuster.
>>62387714
THIS. All of early biz did same and fully agree the new cult of late desperate normies are huge faggots. Their average is literally $15+.
>>62387314
THIS. They know they are wrong and that their thesis is tech illiterate and doesn't even make sense. They know CLL are building out for enterprise success and literally using them as findom liquidity sources that they pay to market headlines based on HUGE exaggerations of "partnerships" to sell to and that they could still choose differently and make it on other projects but they would literally rather signal consensus with the cult and cling to narcissistic ideological fixation like fucking leftists defending shit they know to be evil in a society they know their ideas are not real and will not matter within but they would rather go on a self-immolating rampage than just admit they were wrong or break formation. It is so fucking strange it is hard to imagine being. Like genuinely, I can be very in the dumps but can you IMAGINE being dysfunctional in this way and how horrible and dissonant and distorted as fuck it must be? Nightmare life.

62395753
>>62395728
That hack had zero to do with ZRO's tech. KelpDAO chose to use a single point of failure setup when advised to not. And I am not even shilling Layer Zero or vaguely referencing it. I was talking about the ABSURD price and slowness per CCIP transaction, which you absurdly expect to be just accepted across trillions or payments when it could be fully circumvented and in a structure that also automates the trust into trustless swaps with instant settlement.
>>62387778
Perfectly said.

62395760
>>62395748
>DTCC literally said it will be more than 15 fucking years to rollout the partnered product at Smartcon
They never said this, you made it up.
See the fact that DTCC is going live with Chainlink in Q4 this year.

>>62395753
>KelpDAO chose to use a single point of failure setup
That "single point of failure" was LayerZero's own node.

62395763
>>62383255
This is devastatingly well articulated

62395771
>>62395753
>KelpDAO chose to use a single point of failure setup when advised to not.
kek no, the fault was entirely with the bridge validator, which was operated by layerzero.
>ABSURD price and slowness per CCIP transaction
you don't understand the technical difference between a bridge and a relayer

62395805
Chainlink token (ticker: LINK) will be valued at over $1,000 USD by approximately the end of this calendar year 2026.

62395811
>>62382825
You could have just bought something with a good reason to go up and then you could have been actually rich instead just hoping to be rich. The same goes for today. There are new, fresh moneymaking opportunities every day out there.

62395836
>>62395805
>singles of deceit
>brown id

62395837
>>62383267
if your main business model is drumming up "announcements" or "headlines" for exit liquidity as often as possible, then of course you can find a plethora of instances where those announcements coincide with a btc dump, since btc is dumping about 50% of the time it has ever existed if not more

pricefeeds and whatever else they're doing is basically the side hustle

62395845
>>62395837
>if your main business model is drumming up "announcements" or "headlines" for exit liquidity

What on earth makes you think this is DTCC's main business model?

62395875
>>62395760
No I didn't, they literally said it at Smartcon. The whole stink shilling board was demoralized beyond consoling after anticipating the event as a catalyst that would be positive for them. Lol
>>62395771
Kelpdao opted to use the lowest n setting the software technically allowed rather than putting up training wheels to force companies to choose good security practice, to make it easier and cheaper to run and got rekt. No other client or customer who uses even n+1 what they chose was remotely affected nor were token holders, network function, etc. All of their peers particularly bigger ones did not. If this was a ZRO problem, why was Kelpdao targeted? Could it be because they used egregiously poor security practices? Lol
Again, I am not even shilling fucking ZRO here nor am I shilling any bridge product. You can atomically settle value with micropayment batching for a fraction of a penny in seconds. You are trying to posture erudite but don't even know what they fuck I am talking about and are doubling down attacking a strawman that was never even originally me. I give zero fucks about Layer Zero and if they went bankrupt it would not affect my thesis one bit or change or relate to what I've said at all.

62395906
>>62395875
>No I didn't, they literally said it at Smartcon.
Then explain why DTCC is going live this year, not 15 years from now?

Also you could just post the source since all Smartcon talks are on YT, but you didn't.
Because you made it up.

And LayerZero's own "Decentralized Verifier Network" got hacked. Take two seconds to ingest this very simple information.

62395971
>>62395906
Petulant retard. I literally described exactly what happened in >>62395875. You extremely ironically never took the time to see what actually happened and just desperately splooged at the headline of a project you view to be a competitor having hack attached to it. The quote I mention is fucking common knowledge. I am not going to dig through Chainlink's video repository and scrub for hints that sound familiar to dig up a timestamp to win an argument about something against a petulant retard who is obsessed and literally doesn't care what's true. The DTCC rollout using Link as a service was explicitly mentioned as taking at least 15 years to rollout. That doesn't mean they won't begin tokenizing on other networks (like Canton or XLM) this year.

62396025
>>62395971
>The DTCC rollout using Link as a service was explicitly mentioned as taking at least 15 years to rollout.
And yet the rollout is happening THIS year.
Because you made it up.

That's pretty much all you've been doing these past 6 years: making up and spamming Link fud.

>I literally described exactly what happened
Here's LayerZero themselves:
"our own internal RPCs that the LayerZero Labs DVN used were attacked"
"we made a mistake by allowing our DVN to act as a 1/1 DVN for high-value transactions."

https://layerzero.network/blog/an-overdue-apology

62396233
>>62395760
they did say it, specifically this lady,

but guess what chainlink took that vid down the same day....


>>62395748
smart anon, you are right,

62396238
>>62395906
nope. they removedd the vid, but here is the screenshot of when she was speaking about it


at least i reaspesct this women becuse she told the truth and acceptind millions of link tokens from sergey and still trashed it.

62396246
>>62395341
poor stupid girl has nothing else going on for her life, accepting free salray with out needing to show anything for it...

all the brilliant minds of chainlik labs leaves when they realize its all a grift..

the only ones who stay have nothing else to work for ,

62396260
>>62395805
before i hoped people like yu wouldnt lose everything, tried to get you to see the real picture...


now i hope people like you keep investing in link and lose it all as the decades come ...

enjoy link at .45 cents in 8 years...

i know youll still be buying it..

god ,sergey has it made... lucky bastard is gonna get away with it aint he?

62396263
Reading the walls of inane fuddie rambling will leave a void in my heart one day. Never selling x^)

62396292
>>62396263
whatever you do dont sell... but please keeep buyin it, i stilll have 8k link in a cold wallet i need to get in new york.

duming it next bull run when link hits 14 dollars and youre euephoric,


just like how you were euephoric ehen link was 27 this bull.

62396331
>>62382813
I believe in Chainlink. Now what fud cuck AI copypasta poster?

62396341
>rBIjPT2t
this retard is literally in every thread, desperately begging anons to keep holding until x catalyst, which is ALWAYS something 6-12 months away, and once that deadline finally arrives, he IMMEDIATELY pivots to a brand new carrot, which is again, 6-12 months away
>pls guise DON'T SELL, this year is OUR YEAR!1!!
every
fucking
year
and he's been doing this since 2020
I'm not even sure if he's paid or legit retarded at this point

62396355
>>62396233
>>62396238
>they removedd the vid
lol yeah, they removed the vid just like they removed "all references to staking v1.0" topkek
here it is: https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=7eCrqQs6RiI

Now timestamp where she said this.

62396362
>>62396341
>gets BTFO with all his different fud IDs
>fires up another new ID just to cry like a bitch

tale as old as time

62396382
jeet marines been really quiet since this dropped

https://youtu.be/26N4u0ihIT4?t=12
https://x.com/taodaily_io/status/2064680225577804267

62396420
>s-s-samefag
>w-w-why do you care???
>y-y-you're crying, not me
the insecure little fagit always accuses of you of samefagging, always thread stalks at least 3-4 different threads, and he does that for almost 10-12 hours 4-5 days per week, it is insane really
he took a small hiatus at the beginning of the year, when sentiment was utter dogshit and he hadn't found the new deadline to shill yet, but then returned at full force and has been going nonstop ever since
I'm still not sure if this is a case of legitimate mental ilness or paid shilling, because I honestly fail to see the value of aggresively shilling LINK on /biz/ of all places, unless the team is in ultra desperation mode

62396430
>>62396355
upload the full onne that was 3 hours long, this is the vid where i got my screenshot, '
sergey was on the right side...

this is from the same day tho...

ccant timestamp it since chainlink cut it out.

item.title: Uh? This will have consequences — 62 replies — 248.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
Uh? This will have consequences

Open thread • Replies: 62 • Updated: 2026-06-22 15:01 UTC

OP

https://www.usdebtclock.org/

Are you all just shit coin investors? Did any of you even bother to do the math on this?

Literally, the US is going to enter into debt insolvency at about 55 trillion. That will happen pretty fast.

What does this have to do with business?

Well, the US has lost HALF of it's money's value in the last 25 years. That is going to get exponentially worse.

I get it, twenty years ago, people reeeee'd about "meh hyper inflations"... but seriously, the hour glass is losing sand fast.
|
The public debt will become your problem eventually.

Early replies (first 3)

62384457
wrong

62384460
>>62384445
>This will have consequences
cope

62384462
wen doe

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62387969
>>62384818
im on mobile and my id constantly changes
i imagine this is most posters
just realize this is the world now

62387975
At least fort knox still has gold in it
Geg

62388041
>>62386776
You have Trump Derangement Syndrome. Iran lost.

62388070
>>62388041
>TACO
>b-b-but they lost!
trump doesn't care if you polish his turds
the war was a bad idea on the merits, but this surrender is a fucking joke

62389762
>the US lost half of its moneys value in 25 years

Nigga that's 3% inflation.

62390043
>>62384445
I buy gold everytime it dips, quite literally not my problem.

>muh government muh siezures

I buy guns whenever I'm not buying gold. Otherwise good luck arguing with a bunch of people with normalcy bias. Despite living through half a dozen black swans they remain convinced that nothing ever happens. How many anons are buying SPCX despite it no longer being profitable? They don't care about the future only the current thing. The day after a 3% correction its like it never happened.

62390053
>>62388041
You lost, you gained nothing and spent billions of dollars doing it. If you won ZOG wouldn't be seething about the deal that just fell through. So no only did Iran win round 1 they'll win the next leg as well when the US agrees to an even shittier deal.

62390077
>>62384445
They'll just make some fake money to pay the fake debt. Life will get worse for the average man and the system will keep chugging along. It's all fake.

62391707
>>62384445
>Literally, the US is going to enter into debt insolvency at about 55 trillion.

nigger you didnt understand the money system

62391740
>>62384445
so then we'll just refinance.

62391789
>guys the US debt is about to explode
I've been hearing this for over 25 years

62391883
>>62391707
retard alert

62391889
>>62388041
>US spent billions on air strikes
>Iran closes strait again today
Trump lost. You can buy some Trump merch though.

62391893
>>62389762
based single digit IQ gorilla

62392179
>>62387808
> set it to $0 and watch the servers burn

zero price means zero incentive to fix anything.

62392246
>live on a paradise planet
>man’s greatest achievement is spying on everyone and putting himself in debt to himself

62392281
>>62392179
what needs to be fix'd sir? see >>62387805

62392284
>>62384445
Hopefully. But not for those in the upper arm of the K

62392561
>>62384445
They are probably going to seize every house and sell them like they did for gold to destroy the national debt

62392572
>>62384445
>US is going to enter into debt insolvency
cope

62393851
>>62384445
Japan is at 200% debt to GDP and they are "fine"

62393908
>>62385662
Then turns around with clogged pipes and on fire.

62393910
>>62384445
This is just the retarded libertarian version of climate panicans.

62394032
>>62384788
some reason for a profitable war will be found, don't worry.

62394098
>>62393851
They are importing thousands of jeets

62394128
>>62385662

paper tiger, couldn't even unclog Jeetranian blockage of the straight

62394130
>>62384445
Debt is an amplifier of negativity.. As long as there is good tidings, it won't really matter.

62394363
>>62384445
I have enough to pay off my individual taxpayer obligation 20x over.

Really it shoild be we all get hit for the debt bill and if you cant pay if your citizenship is revoked.

62396241
>>62394098
America has been quite literaly 50% white for a while now lilbro

62396466
>>62394098
irrelevant

item.title: What made you still hold BTC? — 98 replies — 106.8/hr
item.content:encoded:
What made you still hold BTC?

Open thread • Replies: 98 • Updated: 2026-06-22 14:12 UTC

OP

Bitcoin has gone through multiple cycles, crashes, and endless criticism, yet many continue to hold. What keeps your conviction strong after all these years?

Early replies (first 3)

62371733
>>62371718
>What keeps your conviction strong after all these years?

62371807
The only reason to ever sell bitcoin is to buy more at a lower price.

62371917
>>62371807
correct
>waiting for a good entry

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62386979
>>62384208
Nobody knows

62387122
>>62384166
Do you think it will attract a lot of interest in the long run? The model looks good, and since it's self-custodial while expanding Bitcoin's use cases, I don't think it raises the usual concerns that many people have with DeFi.

62387125
>>62383619
Anon is relaxing

62387534
>>62387122
We'll know for sure once TBV launches on mainnet. That's when we'll see if it gains adoption and makes a real impact.

62387549
>>62383732
If you buy memecoins, not $btc

62387563
>>62387534
Defi is no longer valuable

62387566
Im fine

62387833
>>62387563
Depends what you mean by valuable. A lot of the hype is gone, but people are still borrowing, lending, and farming yield.

62387836
>>62387549
Doge coins lol

62387875
>>62371718
Because it's the only crypto asset I'd be comfortable holding for the next decade without constantly checking the chart.

62387892
the longer this market exists, the more Bitcoin seems to become the benchmark everything else gets compared against. So very very bullish

62388642
>>62387875
100%

62388704
>>62371718
Institutions are starting to make it into BTC, before bridge was the issue but with TBV eliminating that, more money will start flowing into BTC, it still feels early.

62388780
>>62376610
> printing is the only thing keeping the bubble from popping instantly. you want that?

62388819
>>62377444
> nothing has changed except the price chart and your cope levels

62388854
nothing, im not retarded so i take profits. my cost basis is negative now.

62389149
>>62388780
You know my answer for that

62389340
I feel like something new has to happen before the market shifts sentiment.

All the ETFs and government regulation just hurt the project.
Nobody is utilizing the tools, the tools still feel beta, and developers are being arrested.

If it's just going to be a big gay digital rock the momentum grinds to a halt.

The problem is some of us got rich and have something to lose now.
Different factions formed inside the developer community.
Is there incentive to free ball the network still? idk.

I'm still holding but this bear feels different

62390164
>>62388819
So how will you change it?

62391118
>>62389340
I'm still holding as well.

62391156
>>62371718
It feels like shit when it goes down but when you're near the bottom like now you just remember the last times you felt this way and how it gongo up from here, everytime. Bobos are ngmi. Mumu supremacy is real. Cope trannies.

62391165
>>62383619
Based retard

62391192
>>62371718
The 20% long-term-capital-gains tax rate that I'd have to pay if I cashed out all at once.

62391471
The only investments I hold are schizo nonsense Chainlink and PMs. I have a good amount of money through my Roth and 401k. I can only invest in specific etfs through my work 401k. What’s the best method to get long term Bitcoin exposure through a IRA?

62392524
>>62391156
You're not a real investor

62393205
>>62372380
Then how come I can send BTC to my Bitcoin core wallet dumbfuck?

62393451
>>62371718
I believe in the philosophy that led to bitcoin. Sadly the vast majority of humanity are just cattle. I still hold because I believe there is something that can happen. The pessimism on this board doesn't change my view on this. I will hold and if it fails I'll be ok because I have stocks and silver and land.

62395366
>>62391192
That's too much to be honest

62395566
>>62371718
I plan on selling when I retire in ten years. But who knows maybe I will change my mind and keep holding.

62396187
All these retards are greedy, that's all there is to it

item.title: Irl crime — 26 replies — 104.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
Irl crime

Open thread • Replies: 26 • Updated: 2026-06-22 14:58 UTC

OP

Share crime stories and currently relevant opportunities ITT. IRL shit, not online scamming.

Inb4
>muh bankers muh politicians
Wow, very funny, what an original joke

Early replies (first 3)

62393319
>>62393307
I fucked your mom.

62393322
12 years ago my first job was in a supermarket. I randomly decided to steal a candy bar for no reason, but i felt so guilty that I confessed to my supervisor a day later and offered to pay. She said it was okay, and nothing happened.

62393326
>>62393319
This guy paid me $50 so he could fuck your mom.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62393330
>>62393319
>>62393326
Guys, guys.. OP is talking about crime, not having legal fun with mothers.

Follow my example for what a real crime is. I am a candybar bandit.

62393331
>>62393326
>>62393322
>>62393319
Gay

62393349
>>62393307
I steal 30 minutes from my employer every day, making it 125,5 hours stolen during the year. That's 5,2 days of free time.

62393351
>>62393331
being gay is not a crime in my cuntry.

62393356
>>62393351
Wow you're such a funny guy

>>62393349
That's hardly crime at all
I meant more like torturing someone for crypto, armed robbery etc

62393424
>>62393307
I didn't do this but I know another guy that did. I worked in a major chain pharmacy while in college and worked with a guy that would have another friend phone in prescriptions pretending to be a certain doctor that often called in to the message line and leave prescriptions on our message recorder (which is legal). Most of the prescriptions were for xanax and such, and he would ring them up himself and then sell them to pill junkies. I only found out about it when I filled in another few stores and saw the same thing there. I asked him about it and he denied it, but I knew and didn't tell anything to anyone.
Anyway he did this for a while and when there was the 10k bitcoin for pizza came out, I remember him being super bullish about it saying he worked 'extra' to buy as many bitcoin as possible. He would even take diabetic test strips and just walk out with them...outright stealing. He would show me them and say '2 boxes equals 1 bitcoin' and laugh at me. I was like wagging my finger and saying 'you'll learn'. Fast forward to 2017 and I ran into him at a gas station literally driving a Lambo. He said I should have listened to him and the last thing he said was 'Crime pays', and that was the last I heard from him.

62393431
>>62393307
I stole a fridge magnet from a cubbyhole at my daycare when I was 5 and I haven't ever not felt guilty about it.
It had courage the cowardly dog on it.

62393475
>>62393424
Now this is a QUALIY post

62393479
>>62393475
QUALITY*

62393519
Bump

62393717
Bump

62393826
>>62393307
I'm sure beds for pigs would be fine if the pigs were providing some kind of valued service

62394022
I stole over 20k worth of gold from my former best friends father's safe because he wrote the fucking code on paper and it was in his nightstand. Drove to neighboring country to sell it for cash. By the time I heard about it enough time had passed that I don't think they ever suspected me. My friend was telling me his dad was pissed and accused their house cleaner lol.

62394033
>>62393307
Find gov depots and ebay their shit. You paid for it.

62395766
I took 64GB of RAM from an unused computer at work and put it in my home computer. I needed it

62395812
>>62393307
wipepo always going around starting bogus wars based on lies, looking for wmd where there aren't any, but meanwhile israel has the wmd, but white trash are quiet about it. they're too scared to even mention it. all problems come from white trash males

62395828
in college (over 20 years ago) I grew mushrooms in a big rubbermaid tote. kept getting insane flushes and had about 6 pounds dry. sold it to an underclassman very cheap, like 120 an ounce. I assume he was just reselling it. blew most of the money helping a friend who was in film school to produce his senior thesis short.

62396109
>>62393424
Nice one. I bet that happens all the time.

When I was in high school in Minecraft everyone wanted shrooms but there was no source for them so I started ordering Aminita muscaria online and sold it to a dealer in bulk. He bought bags of it from me every week for about five weeks and then finally told me my shit sucks and people wanted their money back. I still made thousands and in high school that’s a lot.

62396291
>>62393307
I've unironically heavily considered card fraud considering how easy it is to do and the fact that literal gangbanging hood negroes do it regularly and get at worst a mild punishment for a fraction of the money stolen
I just don't want to get raped and glock dookied in prison so I'm too scared to commit

62396405
>>62395828
How the fuck did you not have mold contaminate the crates??I struggled endlessly with this back in the day

62396415
>>62396291
Pussy

I knew quite a few lads that went into that and made a buck

62396427
>>62396415
>lads
the US is soft on cc fraud but the UK is even more so desu
I know your fraud yutes over there rarely even get time but here they'll get a year or two at least so the government can pretend they're doing something

62396438
>>62393307
I eat poop

item.title: Post your IQ and salary >100 >120k — 101 replies — 74.1/hr
item.content:encoded:
Post your IQ and salary >100 >120k

Open thread • Replies: 101 • Updated: 2026-06-22 13:45 UTC

OP

Post your IQ and salary
>100
>120k

Early replies (first 3)

62391239
>>62391236
135
Currently on gibs, 600 a month

62391240
>>62391236
i dont know my IQ but i assume its just above legally retarded

62391242
>>62391236
We used to joke about data scraping but now I think it might be actually happening.
I see a thread like this once every 5 days.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62392675
>>62391236
>130-135
>$0

62392677
>>62391236
How am I supposed to know my IQ? The real test costs hundreds of dollars

62392741
>>62392675

item.title: >boss put me on a PIP 2 months ago >been trying to find jobs a few months before that cuz I realized there was no pleasi… — 112 replies — 66.3/hr
item.content:encoded:
>boss put me on a PIP 2 months ago >been trying to find jobs a few months before that cuz I realized there was no pleasi…

Open thread • Replies: 112 • Updated: 2026-06-22 13:26 UTC

OP

>boss put me on a PIP 2 months ago
>been trying to find jobs a few months before that cuz I realized there was no pleasing him
>haven't found any jobs, its unreal how bad the market is, have this bad feeling its never going to get better
>PIP ends on Tuesday
I am an engineer. I started off as a tech and worked my ass off getting bachelors full time to escape manual labor. Now im gonna be that goy with a masters degree who took a 500 percent pay cut to do repetitive ligament destroying work. I honestly feel like god did this beacuse its too much bad things happening at once that are culminating into my total demise.

Early replies (first 3)

62388691
>>62388676
>500 percent pay cut to do repetitive ligament destroying work
Trades pay more than ever, especially vs pajeet-saturated office work. Skill issue

62388694
>>62388676
My entire life has been nothing but fighting tooth and nail, working myself 1000x harder than anyone anywhere ever for 0.001% of what anyone else gets for free. I have felt that god is purposely shitting on me pretty much my whole life. It doesn't get better, it only ever gets worse. If anything happens to me that seem to be a good thing, I have 6 months max to enjoy it because that'll be taken from me and then I'll be beaten up and crushed down for added effect. It's a real comedy out there.

So don't worry bro, you have a lot of time ahead of you to suffer far more. If there really is a god, he must be the most perfectly evil of entities.

62388697
The worst part is that in my meeting with my boss today he didnt mention if I will pass the PIP. He said he needed to talk with some people including HR. So 90 percent chance he is going to fire me but just enough chance he wont that I have to spend the weekend waiting instead of processing the pain. And if he doesnt fire me, I will be a totally buckbroken employee who will never care about a rasie and work 60 hours weeks. Like a absurd housewife that has no were else to go and she tries to esapce and gets rapped(applies to ghost jobs) even worse so she comes back to him. Thats me. I used to think my relationship with work was like between a man and a woman, were I am the man that picks and chooses women and is the one who leaves but its all turned around past couple of years. I am nothing but a side pieace female whore to these companies now, they treat me like a contract worker without paying me like one. They got us with not giving raises to match inflation, they got us with return to work mandates and now, they are going to get us by firing us and leaving us with nothing. U will get put on a PIP too, at the worst time possible also. They wont even lay u off anymore, they want to save a little bit of money by denying us unemployment insurance by firing us through a PIP.


Was being in ur 30s a good excperince back in the day? There is no way human civilization would have gotten to were it is if it was always this bad for everyone. Of course, I am a faggot and just suck but the fsct of the matter is that PIPs and no jobs has been a very common trope on the internet lately

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62390565
>>62388697
>they treat me like a contract worker without paying me like one.
Holy FUCK that's what I've felt too.

Some people are allowed to stay solid and secure because of their personal connections and the rest of us get bait and switched during the interviews, squeezed of what they can get out of us for a while, then tossed out when the knowledge transfer is done or the project is good enough for them to hand it to one of their buddies and take it from you. You get worked like a slave and then cast out. Chewed up and spit out.

62390573
>>62388795
>Just make sure they fire you so you can get unemployment.
I thought it was if you get fired you don't get unemployment.
I someone tried pip'ing me too but I was protected by a higher up who realized that politics was fucking my shit up with a hostile environment so he mercy killed me with a layoff so I got a nice severance. After that I also got unemployment for my state.

62390675
>>62388691
>NAH MAN MY DAD'S BOYFRIEND'S STEP DOG'S COUSIN'S BROTHER IS AN ELECTRICIAN AND HE MAKES $370K A YEAR WORKS 3 MONTHS EVERY OTHER YEAR DOING UNDERWATER DATA CENTER CONSTRUCTION IN ALASKA NO DEBT BTFO COLLAGE FAGS

62391179
>>62389426
Mmm yes, go to the meat grinder goym

62391366
>>62390565
And then all these boomer managers judging me in interviews telling me ive been bouncing around all over the place, its awful

62391513
>>62390249
>she still thinks satanists are in it for the edge alone
>thinking fascism is edgy, an authoritarian imposition of traditional morals

62391843
>>62389431
I'd welcome you to try, sukdeep dikshit. I'll break every bone in your body if you come at me with a saw. Have you ever even operated a saw?

62391855
>>62388729
>>62388736
Retards, mostly 2nd anon.

You get off of backbreaking shit after jman(4-6 years) and specialize. Or sales, or foreman, or estimators, or inspectors, or maint.

Low IQ stay in back breaking

62391907
>>62390573
No, you get unemployment only if you are laid off due to economic constraints or incompetence. Thats why PIPs aren’t that bad. You don’t get unemployment if you are fired for negligence (this can be fought if you have proof they set you up or if they were playing favorites) or if you leave willingly. Unemployment benefits tend to pay up to 2000 dollars a month depending on your salary.
>t. Corporate wagie who dealt with it before

62392212
>>62391843
And who might you be? How about you learn to speak only when spoken to.

62392253
>>62388711
>ah yes did i tell you? all women around me have fancy jobs with big salaries and 100% remote positions and company trips all over the world
the new system is in place permanently and working as intended

62392282
>>62388711
>but i refuse to do it because my gf would earn more than me
Yeah, that’s a very good reason. Never let a girl out earn you.
>t. retired at 25

62392283
>>62392253
> >ah yes did i tell you? all women around me have fancy jobs with big salaries and 100% remote positions and company trips all over the world
Doesn’t bother me. Retired at 25. I know how damaging a 9-5 is and how even waking up at 7am is shitty and terrible for your healthy. Going to bed with an alarm is terrible too. No girl is out earning me. Trust me.

62393076
>>62388802
>I see a nig or a nog here and there
Kek

62393091
>>62388992
Kys

I'm in a very comfy sport right now but most of my life has been a shitshow ripe with suffering. I'll be "grateful" when I'm dead. Until then it's hate, hate, hate and a lot of grit

62393160
>>62388730
May everyone you love die of cancer before your eyes.

62393171
>>62388761
Kys

62393407
>>62388802
This post PROUDLY presented by a jeet.

62393484
>>62391855
Ye bro, let me just causally go from doing interesting work to hammering in nails to build more houses for the jeet and beaner invaders that the boomers are gonna rent out to

62393491
>>62391907
Wait wait wai. I've been tearing my hair out cuz I dont think I will get bennies beacuse i am on a PIP and ur telling me i can still get them? I called the unemployment office and they said 50/50 so idk man. Would be nice

62393565
>>62393091
This is something I have to come to terms with as I get older. My luck isnt the worst b u t the people in my life, especially my biological family has been making.my life hell and I need to cut ties with them once my grandma dies. All the bad things that happened to me in life could have been way easier to deal with without my family making it worse.

62393627
>>62392212
silly esl. you spoke to me and I spoke back. glad you adjusted your energy to match your grip strength, bitch.

62394155
>>62388676
Is that Matteo or Matthew?

62395199
>>62393091
You should be grateful. If not for your suffering, you wouldn't be a successful man. Forgive your mother for making you think life should be like you are a woman.

62395202
>>62393160
I'll still be grateful for what they gave me. Learn to stop acting like an entitled woman. Karen.

62395616
>>62388778
>went to the worst liberal shithole of europe
>why am I struggling??

62395641
>>62388676
>people actually get put on PIPs
bro, how? I have so many workers in my department that don't try for shit at anything. literally nothing happens to them ever. corporate america has some fucked up shit, but generally I've seen the PIP system be less abused than other bullshit

62395717
>>62395641
Not every corporation runs the same
Shocking

62395927
My dream is, if my current gig works out (which will leave me with a lot of cash), is to start a software shop where the work-day is capped at 4 days. I don't care about making it big, I just want to spend my time at work with other people who are happy and live decent enough lives. Since it's an unexploited niche I wager I can gather some pretty decent talent.

Fuck this race to the bottom, I don't wanna be rich I wanna be happy

62395938
>>62395717
>>62395641

It's actually more about your boss. 90% of bosses don't gaf as long as you generally do what they say. There is a 10% of proactive dicks who are absolutely looking to roll heads.

item.title: missed SHIB and all the other 1million% shitcoin gainers. — 7 replies — 28.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
missed SHIB and all the other 1million% shitcoin gainers.

Open thread • Replies: 7 • Updated: 2026-06-22 14:58 UTC

OP

missed SHIB and all the other 1million% shitcoin gainers. now there's none left, every thought/idea/meme has been created twice over and nothing is ever shilled here anymore

Early replies (first 3)

62393144
>>62393112
You missed shib but you were born right on time to buy a used pressure washer off Facebook marketplace and start a small business.

62393246
>>62393112
It is mathematically possible for JasmyCoin (JASMY) to reach $1, but it is considered highly unlikely in the foreseeable future. Reaching $1 would require a massive shift in market dynamics, exponential adoption, and a staggering market capitalization.

To understand why $1 is difficult, you have to look at the token supply. Jasmy has a circulating supply of roughly 49.3 billion tokens. At a price of $1, JasmyCoin's market capitalization would reach approximately $49.3 billion.'
A $49+ billion market cap would place Jasmy among the top 10 most valuable cryptocurrencies in the world, requiring it to surpass established assets like Solana or XRP. Because of this supply hurdle, most financial and crypto analysts provide much more conservative price targets, with few predicting it to reach $1 in the short or medium term.

Major prediction platforms like Changelly and CoinCodex project Jasmy's value in the range of $0.02 to $0.10 over the next few years. Even optimistic platforms like Coinpedia estimate average prices around $0.18 to $0.27 by 2030.

62393409
>>62393144
> pressure washer
Can't anyone shill a business idea that isn't already plastered all over tiktok/hypersaturated to hell?

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62396223
>>62393112
Sometimes I daydream what it wouldve been like if I had bought some of that shit and held. I automatically assumed the first memecoins were all pajeet scams from SHIB, HOGE, Safemoon and so on. All shilled on biz before 10000-100000x'ing. Nothing like it left today, biz is a barren wasteland and all easy crypto money has been milked dry by cliques pumping n dumping.
Kudos to whoever made it out

62396345
>>62396223
Let’s try and make a biz gem. Obviously this has been tried and failed 1000x, but why not give it a whirl. Let’s start with a tg and see if we can come up with ideas

62396394
>>62396345

just post your idea here or it's gonna be the same 3 dudes shilling their dead projects by day 2

62396435
>>62396345

>we

How about I do stuff that helps me and you get bent

62389128

He looks like a bum, jeez

62389578
>>62388705

62389648
>>62388826
Is chainlink on their chain yet?

62389663
>>62388673
Glad to see hes also balding just like his bagholders like me. Piece of fucking shit

62389677
>>62389648
no

62390366
>>62388673
When people understand the big picture of what is happening across the entire Western world instead of just viewing everything at the individual level they will understand why he's right.
They are collapsing public services and they're going to privatise everything from the police to the army, education and NASA. The running down of all of these institutions is deliberate and designed to force people to look for privately owned solutions.
All of this is going ahead whether people like it or not because the alternative is a global revolution and the people are too dumb, lazy and disorganised to attempt it.

62390408
>>62388673
not even link holders use ccip
no one uses blockchain, defi, all that crap is worthless

62391531
>>62390408
did you check defillama's revenue table recently?

62393490
>>62391531

62393492
>>62388673

62393595
Im buying 7500 GLNK in my IRA tomorrow. Try and talk me out of it

62393623
>jeetlink thread on the first page

62393645
>>62393595
worst case is that you lose your money while everyone else is making steady gains on stocks, so i say go for it. it's your money

62393653
>>62393645
Everything else i look at is so pumped already. Everyone's portfolio i look at is full of AI shit. I already have exposure to all that noise. Chainlink and Gamestop are my only holdings that aren't pumped up like crazy right now. Chainlink in particular is due for a good rebound

62393746
>>62393595
I've been loading my HSA with glnk, so yeah go for it anon.

62393862
>>62393653
>Due for a pump
Why? Everyone that gets involved with it that isn't Sergey gets covered in shit. Look at what happened to the former CTO of SWIFT. Children usually learn to not touch a hot stove after burning themselves twice, even the mentally retarded learn this around the 9th or 10th time. Meanwhile, linkies have been getting burned for 5 straight years and still haven't learned a thing lmao.

62393925
>>62393862
What happened to the CTO?

62394776
>>62390366
>They are collapsing public services and they're going to privatise everything from the police to the army, education and NASA. The running down of all of these institutions is deliberate and designed to force people to look for privately owned solutions.
what is the point of paying taxes then if they're not going to do anything useful for us.

62394797
>>62393595

62394807
>>62394776
You must be 18 years old to post here buddy. They’ll throw you in prison if you aren’t funding Jewish wars and genocide. No social cohesion due to (((diversity))), bread and circus (sports ball and gambling) stops the goy cattle from organizing and revolting. Grim desu

62394831
price eod July 4th?!

62394850
>>62393925

>What happened to the CTO?
wish the question would get answered, but I doubt it
some ai chainlink stuff, wonder if kemal is involved I dunno

62394998
>>62394850

Lmao.

62395151
>>62394998
>muh prestige
>muh x retards
I'm not buying it

62395224
>>62388723
Way sexier then you

62395987
>>62388673

CCIP is COMPLETELY uncompetitive which is why Tom Schach was fired for failing to foresee and plan for literally the only thing he has had to to prevent his company from losing the literally biggest moat since Blockbuster.

item.title: Crypto-Hopium — 10 replies — 10.1/hr
item.content:encoded:
Crypto-Hopium

Open thread • Replies: 10 • Updated: 2026-06-22 14:08 UTC

OP

>The CLARITY Act is about to be signed.
US corporations will begin buying large quantities of Bitcoin and altcoins.
>The US Strategic Bitcoin Reserve will accumulate 5% of the total supply using a rebalanced Strategic Gold Reserve as collateral.
Trump is about to audit Fort Knox, and ARMA is being drafted to prevent future presidents from selling for a minimum of 20 years.
>The housing market has introduced cryptocurrency backed mortgages.
Participants will hold their cryptocurrencies for 15 and 30 year terms.

Early replies (first 3)

62393159
>The CLARITY Act is about to be signed

really nigga? I thought this shit was dead for this year

62393165
>>62393089
Clarity Act isn't going through. It's completely stalled in negotiations and they've got a week to get something like 10 dems to cross lines.

62393203
>>62393165
According to whom?

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62393204
>>62393089
Funny how you niggers always desperately need a meme date/event/regulation/narrative, this board is 99% indians who cannot think for themselves.

62393377
>>62393204
It's funnier how you can read the OP and disregard it as if it has zero positive implications for the cryptocurrency market.

62394871
>>62393089
The government won’t buy anything until everyone capitulated nigger. I’m almost certain it will dump and a final capitulation will begin.

62394904
>>62393203
the recess begins on june 29 and runs to july 10. after that they once again have only 20 days to pass it before the august 1 recess that goes into september, which is midterm season.
>10 dems
the bill does have opponents and it isn't filibuster-proof without some dems voting it in. plus it needs to be bipartisan or it's going to be doa regardless. every dem that has signaled support has done so contingent on some kind of concession, few of which have been addressed

62394929
>>62393089
Trump was meant to be the crypto president and the first thing he did when he took office was rugpull his followers with a shitcoin, ruining whatever goodwill crypto might have had at that point. It's not happening. It's over.

62395050
>>62393204

we need the banks to buy the coins that kill the banks

it's easy, see?

62396159
>>62393089
Cryptobros just don't understand that 10/10 was curtain call for crypto

item.title: Why should I max out a Roth IRA and not just put 20% in that and 80% into buying calls 6+ months out and/or selling puts… — 15 replies — 6.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
Why should I max out a Roth IRA and not just put 20% in that and 80% into buying calls 6+ months out and/or selling puts…

Open thread • Replies: 15 • Updated: 2026-06-22 12:38 UTC

OP

Why should I max out a Roth IRA and not just put 20% in that and 80% into buying calls 6+ months out and/or selling puts?

Early replies (first 3)

62394825
You should buy 0DTE OTM options

62394847
>>62394796
This is AI. Even people who do free climbing like this don't (can't) climb on smooth surfaces for that length.

62394859
>>62394847
Its not ai. Its a French freeclimber Alexis landing.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62394874
Because you don't know what you're doing and your expected rate of return into options plays will be lower than whatever safe shit is in your IRA.

62394882
>>62394874
I made 33% last year only selling puts otm. Much better than my 457b.

62394884
>>62394882
Oh cool. I guess that one year metric means you're good to go and will on average make more money than your IRA. Go for it!

62394897
>>62394884
I guess you're right. Its hard to see when you've only won. I only do it on profitable and safer companies like WMT and NVO etc. As well but any company can have massive downsides. I just want to retire before I'm well past my prime • •

62394934
>>62394796
Looks like a big garloid mating with a small garloid

62394944
>>62394897
All I'm saying is don't get too carried away with gambling based on recent historical results. It's probably fine to be a bit more aggressive than the average normie but a good amount of shmucks with the mindset you have think they are invincible and end up losing half their net worth in 5 years. Just be careful anon.

62394995
>>62394847
looks like there's a few videos of this guy as >>62394859 says. might be legit

62395174
>>62394796
Options are a zero sum game. For you to win, someone else has to lose, and you are up against people who are the best in the world at it and have vast computing and research assets at their disposal. It is hard to beat the forever tax-free gains of the Roth IRA/401k.

62395208
>>62394847
>>62394859
I love it when arrogant faggots get immediately buttblasted

62395712
You can just put the calls in your roth and have no tax on gains

62395776
>>62394796
Aww hell nawww.

62395792
>>62394796
man these videos always make my balls get sweaty

62391414

oh shit

62391425
Hurry quick patent the design and idea before elon musk becomes a richer aire

62391435
>>62391401

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62391441
>>62391401
Invest in ETF $GLUG

62392341
>>62391401
Imagine putting your dick there for a nice lil sucky sucky.

62392351
>>62392341
Imagine not making such pathetic posts

62392483
>>62392351
Why? I am here for trolling fags.

62395998
I thought Olga Kurylenko was Asian or Wasian, because she has soft, feminine features; but apparently she is not. Thanks for making me look into this.

OP

62393798
Bitch i got them genital warts

62393820
>>62393740
same m8

62393909
it's digital silver

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62394086
>>62393740
condolenses!

62394738
>>62393740
I'm slurping but losing hope

62395600
>>62394738
Why? At the very least it seems to be capable of tracking inflation. Look at its previous atl in 2018 and what usd debasement was back then compared to now
